input.title: Banking Application
input.description: Banking Application is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Java Spring Boot, Oracle DB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Account Opening
requirements: Online application for savings and checking accounts
KYC document upload and verification
Initial deposit via card or transfer
Account number generated upon approval

FR-02.title: Fund Transfer
requirements: Transfer between own accounts
Transfer to other bank accounts via routing number
Instant transfers within same bank
Scheduled and recurring transfers

FR-03.title: Bill Payment
requirements: Pay utility bills from account
Save frequent billers for quick access
Schedule recurring bill payments
Payment confirmation and receipt

FR-04.title: Transaction History
requirements: View all transactions with date, amount, and type
Filter by date range, type, and amount
Search transactions by description
Export statement as PDF or CSV

FR-05.title: Two-Factor Authentication
requirements: OTP via SMS or authenticator app for login
OTP required for fund transfers above threshold
Biometric authentication option
Recovery codes for lost device

FR-06.title: Card Management
requirements: View linked debit and credit cards
Freeze and unfreeze cards instantly
Set spending limits per transaction and daily
Report lost or stolen cards

FR-07.title: Loan Application
requirements: Apply for personal loan with amount and tenure
System calculates EMI based on interest rate
Upload income documents for verification
Track application status

FR-08.title: Account Alerts and Notifications
requirements: Balance alerts for low balance threshold
Transaction alerts for debits above set amount
Login alerts for unrecognized devices
Custom alert preferences

FR-09.title: Budget and Spending Analytics
requirements: Categorize transactions automatically
Monthly spending breakdown by category
Budget setting per category with alerts
Spending trends over time

FR-10.title: Customer Support
requirements: In-app chat with support agents
Call back request feature
FAQ and help articles section
Complaint tracking with reference number

FR-01.title: Design Account Opening UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for account opening. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for account opening is complete and reviewable.
The account opening design is responsive and accessible.
The design for account opening aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Account Opening
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for account opening. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Online application for savings and checking accounts; KYC document upload and verification; Initial deposit via card or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required account opening user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for account opening are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for account opening is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Account Opening
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for account opening. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Online application for savings and checking accounts; KYC document upload and verification; Initial deposit via card or transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for account opening is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for account opening enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for account opening is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Account Opening
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for account opening. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for account opening supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for account opening are defined correctly.
Schema changes for account opening can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Account Opening
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for account opening. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for account opening enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for account opening are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of account opening satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Testing Account Opening Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for account opening. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for account opening.
Security, validation, and permission checks for account opening are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important account opening behaviors.
